text: Reproduce it: `sudo queue-workerctl start` prints 'already running (pid 31337)'. Is PID 31337 real? `ps -p 31337` (empty) or `sudo kill -0 31337` (fails: No such process).
The pid comes from a file. `cat /var/run/queue-worker.pid` is 31337 — a leftover from before the power-off. The control script trusts the file's existence and never checks if the process is alive.
Remove the stale pidfile, then start: `sudo rm /var/run/queue-worker.pid && sudo queue-workerctl start`. Confirm with `pgrep -af queue-worker`.
